channel.title: SIR Model on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in SIR Model on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Ordinary Differential Equations (14): Epidemic Models and Epidemiology
item.description: In early 2020 the entire world watched a small system of ordinary differential equations decide policy. “Flatten the curve” was not a slogan; it was the intuition of a specific equation. Herd immunity was not a guess; it was the threshold $1 - 1/R_0$ derived in a single line. The SIR model — four lines of math, written down in 1927 by Kermack and McKendrick — turned out to be precise enough to drive trillion-dollar decisions.
